title: `--opt isolate` causes error during `podman network create`
body:
### Issue Description

Recently upgraded podman to `6.0.0`. The script I used to create an isolated bridge network stopped working; relevant documentation:

- https://docs.podman.io/en/v6.0.0/markdown/podman-network-create.1.html#opt-o-option
   - `isolate`

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue:

1. `podman network create --internal --driver bridge --disable-dns --opt isolate somename`

### Describe the results you received

`Error: netavark (exit code 1): invalid isolate option ""`

### Describe the results you expected

Aforementioned documentation mentions this option and how to invoke it (seems unchanged). I found a PR w/ comment (see below); if I am understanding correctly, `--opt isolate` is now the default? 

Maybe all that needs to happen is for the documentation to be updated?

### Additional information

Potentially relevant comment from recent PR:
- https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/issues/27349#issuecomment-4254913428

comments.body:
The issue is that `--opt isolate` is parsed as key: `isolate` and value: `""`. In [this PR](https://github.com/podman-container-tools/container-libs/pull/610), the call to [`ParseIsolate()`](https://github.com/podman-container-tools/container-libs/blame/381e408771592392771a2552d87dab92b3a51029/common/libnetwork/internal/util/parse.go#L39) was removed. Previously, that translated `--opt isolate` to key: `isolate` and value: `false`. I think when the user uses `--opt isolate`, it should be set to `true`. There is also a significant documentation gap here, in my opinion.

I think it should behave this way:

- `--opt isolate` -> `true`
- `--opt isolate=true` -> `true`
- `--opt isolate=false` -> `false`
- `--opt isolate=strict` -> `strict`
- `--opt` (no isolate) -> `strict`

The root cause is that an empty string is not `None`. It is still a string. See [netavark PR #1438](https://github.com/containers/netavark/pull/1438/changes#diff-1666c106afa4cf84f6285d81e1497b38cd714106564f0bce648e748b0094a422R1150-R1154).

There are two options for fixing this: fix it at the Podman level, where the empty string is translated to `true` or `false`, or fix it in netavark to accept an empty string as `true` or `false`.


WDYT? @Luap99 @ashley-cui

This seems like a documentation issue to me. Options are always supposed to be `key=value`.
If we accepted other syntax before I am fine merging PRs that restore this on the podman side. Otherwise I would say this is an breaking change as part of podman 6 and rather not allow `--opt isolate`
